name: comprehensive-test-suite-structure
description: Organized test suite into 4 categories: tests/unit/ (provider abstractions with mocked AWS), tests/integration/ (API endpoints via TestClient), tests/e2e/ (full pipelines), tests/terraform/ (infrastructure validation). Use pytest markers for selective execution. Minimal mocking philosophy: only mock network boundaries.

content: Test file naming: test_<module>_<component>.py in organized subdirectories. Provider interface contract tests in tests/test_provider_interfaces.py at root level. Use descriptive test class names (TestEmbeddingProviderFactory, TestModalityType) and docstrings for every test.

Clean up broken tests by deletion rather than fixing when they reference obsolete architecture. 10 test files deleted that imported non-existent 'frontend'/'backend' modules from pre-refactor architecture. Improves test collection from 408 tests with 18 errors to 600 tests with 12 errors (unrelated).
